title: Doric Weekly Market Insight
summary:
"While global stock markets continue to anticipate a "soft landing" and remain at their highest levels, the price of copper is following closely Baltic indices on their downward trend"
By Michalis Voutsinas
With Baltic Capesize indices in a freefalling mode, the gauge of activity in the dry bulk sector didn't have any other choice but to report losses during the twenty-first week of the year. After steaming for eleven trading days north of the 3000-point-mark and touching five-month highs, Baltic Dry Index trended downwards to 2681 points on this Friday's closing. Whilst Baltic indices were losing their recent highs, market uncertainty was on a rise.
